metal.runtime_error: thread panicked at cubecl-std-0.9.0/src/fast_math.rs:100:22: attempt to divide by zero
metal.root_cause: cubecl-std 0.9.0 slice_assign_kernel receives a zero tensor dimension in its FastDivmod slice_shape argument during Tensor::cat on the Metal backend. FastDivmod::find_params_u32(0) panics at integer division. The compilation fix (commit a926941) is complete — model structs are now generic over ActiveBackend. The runtime failure is in cubecl-std 0.9.0's slice_assign kernel which computes magic-number divisors for indexing and panics when any slice dimension is zero. Exact zero-dimension source not yet identified; candidates are (1) a tensor produced by an intermediate Metal kernel operation with an unexpected stride, (2) a degenerate batch dimension from argmax/cat interaction on Metal, or (3) a cubecl 0.9.0 Metal-specific bug in the LinearView computation for slice_assign.
metal.fix_estimate: Patch cubecl-std 0.9.0 to trace which tensor dimension is zero, or upgrade to a cubecl release that fixes Metal slice_assign.

note: wgpu measured 2026-04-13 on Apple M-series, --profile bench (no LTO), euler_steps=4, 10 phrases. Metal compilation fixed 2026-04-13 (commit a926941 — all Wgpu type refs replaced with ActiveBackend). Metal runtime failure discovered same day: cubecl-std 0.9.0 panics in slice_assign FastDivmod with divisor=0 during Tensor::cat.
conclusion: Metal compiles clean as of commit a926941 but fails at runtime due to a cubecl-std 0.9.0 bug in the Metal slice_assign kernel. The wgpu WGSL shader path (RTF avg 6.63x) remains the only working GPU path on macOS. Auto-disable threshold (5.0x) is NOT met — TTS remains disabled on this hardware until the Metal runtime bug is resolved.
